---
title: "outcome is Enterococcus"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-03"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(brms)   
library(tidybayes)
library(ggtext)
library(wesanderson)
library(broom.mixed) 
library(cowplot)
library(furrr)
library(dendextend)
library(ggdendro)
```

```{r}
meta <- read_csv('../data/R59_meta_expanded.csv') |> 
  mutate(intensity = factor(intensity, levels = c('nonablative','reduced','ablative'))) %>%
  mutate(pid = factor(pid)) %>%
    # This divides all the new intake columns by 100.
  mutate(across(starts_with("fg_"), ~ .x / 100))

key <- read_csv('../data/food_group_color_key_final.csv', col_types = 'ccccc')

asv_relab_genus <- read_csv('../data/171_quality_asv_relab_pident97_genus.csv')

clr_res <- read_csv('../data/171_genus_CLR_res.csv')

palette <- wes_palette("Royal1", 2)
```

# ASV1 as outcome

```{r}
all_food_vars <- meta %>% select(starts_with('fg')) %>% colnames()

# Create the interaction terms for all food variables in this iteration
interaction_terms <- paste(all_food_vars, "empirical", sep = "*")

# Build the full formula string dynamically
formula_string <- paste(
  "asv_1_clr ~ 0 + intensity + empirical + TPN + EN  +",
  paste(interaction_terms, collapse = " + "),
  "+ (1 | pid) + (1 | timebin)"
)

# Convert the string to a formula object
formula <- brms::bf(as.formula(formula_string))

# Build the priors by adding them together.
# This single prior() call applies to all coefficients listed in `all_food_coefs`.
priors <-
    prior(normal(0, 1), class = 'b') + # General prior for all food effects
    # Specific priors that override the general one for non-food covariates
    prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "TPNTRUE") +
    prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "ENTRUE") +
    prior(normal(0, 0.5), class = 'b', coef = "empiricalTRUE") 

#Fit the model (using fewer iterations for this example to run quickly)
entero_asv1_model <- brm(
  formula = formula,
  data = meta,
  prior = priors,
    warmup = 1000, iter = 3000,
    chains = 4, cores = 4,
    seed = 123,
    silent = 2,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99)
)

results_df <- tidy(entero_asv1_model, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(conf.low = round(conf.low, 2),
         conf.high = round(conf.high, 2)) |> 
  mutate(
    n_samples = nrow(meta),
    n_patients = n_distinct(meta$pid),
  )

results_df |> write_csv('../data/R63_results_df_asv1.csv')
```

## forest asv1 

```{r}
replacement_dictionary <- setNames(key$shortname, key$fg1_name)

level_order <- rev(c(
    "abx", "EN", "TPN", 
    "abx * Sweets", "Sweets",
    "abx * Grains", "Grains",
    "abx * Milk", "Milk",
    "abx * Eggs", "Eggs",
    "abx * Legumes", "Legumes",
    "abx * Meats", "Meats",
    "abx * Fruits", "Fruits",
    "abx * Oils", "Oils",
    "abx * Vegetables", "Vegetables"
))

cleaned_effects <- results_df %>%
  # Keep only the fixed effects
  filter(effect == "fixed") %>%
  # Create a new column to distinguish main effects from interactions
  mutate(
    effect_type = if_else(str_detect(term, ":"), "Interaction", "Main Effect"),
    # Create clean labels for plotting
    clean_term = term %>%
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE$", "abx") %>%
      str_remove_all("empiricalFALSE:|avg_intake_|TRUE$") %>%
      str_replace_all( replacement_dictionary) %>%
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE:", "abx * ") %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ") 
  ) %>%
  filter(!str_detect(clean_term, 'intensity')) %>%
  # Create a new column to identify significant results
  # The condition is TRUE if conf.low and conf.high have the same sign (i.e., don't cross zero)
  mutate(is_significant = (conf.low * conf.high) > 0) %>%
  mutate(clean_term = factor(clean_term, levels = level_order))

shading_df <- cleaned_effects %>%
  mutate(y_numeric = as.numeric(clean_term)) %>%
  filter(str_detect(clean_term, "\\*")) # Filter for interaction terms

plot_asv1 <- ggplot(cleaned_effects, aes(x = estimate, y = clean_term)) +
  geom_rect(
    data = shading_df,
    aes(ymin = y_numeric - 0.5, ymax = y_numeric + 0.5, xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf),
    fill = "#FBEADC", # A light orange/peach color like the example
    alpha = 0.7,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
   ) +
   # Add a vertical line at zero, which represents "no effect"
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "blue",size = 0.8) +
   # Use geom_pointrange to show the estimate (point) and confidence interval (line)
   geom_pointrange(
   aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high, color = is_significant),
     size = 0.25,linewidth = 1,
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "TRUE" = "red",
      "FALSE" = "black"
    ), name = "Effect Status" # Legend title
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete( labels = function(x) { ifelse(str_detect(x, '\\*'),  str_glue("<b style='color:royalblue'>{x}</b>"), as.character(x)) }) +
  # Add labels and a clean theme
  labs(
    x = "CLR E.faecium change",
    y = ''
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.y = element_markdown(), axis.text=element_text(size=10),axis.title=element_text(size=10),
         aspect.ratio=1.3)

plot_asv1
```
```{r}
ggsave('../data/F4b_R63_forest_entero_asv1.pdf',
  width = 90, 
  height = 140, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf', plot = plot_asv1, dpi = 300)  
```

## marginal effects asv1

```{r}
# Pulling the conditional effects directly from the model to build the smooth curves
raw_conditional <- conditional_effects(entero_asv1_model, surface = TRUE)

# Filtering down to just the interactions between food groups and antibiotics
condi_dat <- raw_conditional[str_detect(names(raw_conditional), ":empirical")] |> 
  bind_rows(.id = 'grp') |> 
  mutate(grp = str_replace(grp, '\\:empirical','')) |> 
  mutate(
    # Stretching the x-axis back to normal grams for readability on the final plot
    effect1__ = effect1__ * 100,
    across(starts_with("fg_"), ~ .x * 100) 
  ) |> 
  left_join(key |> select(grp = fg1_name, shortname)) %>% 
  mutate(shortname = factor(shortname, levels = rev(c(
                 'Vegetables', 'Oils', 'Fruits', 'Meats',
                 'Legumes', 'Eggs', 'Milk', 'Grains', 'Sweets'))))

conditional_plots <- condi_dat |>   
  ggplot() +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(x = effect1__, y = estimate__, ymin = lower__, ymax = upper__, fill = effect2__, color = effect2__),
    stat = "identity", alpha = .3, linewidth = 1.5
  ) + 
    scale_fill_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  scale_colour_manual('antibiotics', values = palette, labels=c('not exposed', 'exposed')) +
  facet_wrap(~ shortname, nrow = 3, scales = 'free_x') +
  labs(y = 'Predicted CLR(E. faecium)', x = 'Food group consumed (grams)') +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = 'top', legend.title = element_text(size = 8, face="bold"),
        legend.text = element_text( size=8, face="bold"),
        legend.background=element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0)),
       legend.key=element_rect(fill = alpha("white", 0)),
        aspect.ratio = 1, 
        strip.background = element_rect(color="white", fill='#ffecdc', size=1.5,linetype="solid"))    +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(direction = "vertical"), color = guide_legend(direction = "vertical"))

conditional_plots
```

```{r}
ggsave('../data/E07e_R63_conditional9_entero_asv1.pdf',
  width = 150, 
  height = 150, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf', plot = conditional_plots, dpi = 300)  
```

# genus as outcome

```{r}
df_relab <- asv_relab_genus |> 
  group_by(sampleid, genus) |>
  summarize(
    relab = sum(count_relative, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  filter(sampleid %in% meta$sampleid) 

# I want to only include the genus that are more prevalent and abundant, and most likely exist in human gut
# 31 left
target_genera <-  df_relab %>% 
  group_by(genus) %>% 
  count(relab > 0.002) %>% 
  rename(criteria = names(.)[2]) %>% 
  filter(criteria == 'TRUE') %>% 
  arrange(-n) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(genus) ) %>% 
  filter(!genus %in% c('Ruthenibacterium', 'Drancourtella')) |> 
  mutate(perc = round(n/nrow(meta)*100, 0)) %>% 
  filter(perc > 10) %>% 
  pull(genus)

# joining with the clr of the genera
clr_wide <- clr_res %>% 
  filter(genus %in% target_genera) |> 
  spread('genus','clr')

meta_genus_clr <- meta %>% 
  inner_join(clr_wide)  
```


```{r}
# Extract the names of the taxa to be modeled as outcomes.
# This grabs columns from 'Actinomyces' to the end of the dataframe.
taxa_outcomes <- meta_genus_clr |> 
  select(Actinomyces:last_col()) |> 
  colnames()

# Define the right-hand side of the formula once to avoid repeating work.
all_food_vars <- meta_genus_clr |> 
  select(starts_with('fg')) |> 
  colnames()

interaction_terms <- paste(all_food_vars, "empirical", sep = "*")

base_rhs <- paste(
  "0 + intensity + empirical + TPN + EN +",
  paste(interaction_terms, collapse = " + "),
  "+ (1 | pid) + (1 | timebin)"
)

# Define the priors once.
model_priors <- 
  prior(normal(0, 1), class = 'b') + 
  prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "TPNTRUE") +
  prior(normal(0, 0.1), class = 'b', coef = "ENTRUE") +
  prior(normal(0, 0.5), class = 'b', coef = "empiricalTRUE") 

# Create a wrapper function that takes a single outcome name, 
# dynamically builds the formula, and fits the model.
fit_taxa_model <- function(outcome, data, rhs, priors) {
  
  # Construct the full formula for this specific outcome
  formula_string <- paste(outcome, "~", rhs)
  model_formula <- brms::bf(as.formula(formula_string))
  
  # Fit the model using the cmdstanr backend for massive speed improvements.
  # Cores is set to 1 here because parallelization will happen at the model level,
  # not the chain level, to avoid overloading the CPU.
  fit <- brm(
    formula = model_formula,
    data = data,
    prior = priors,
    warmup = 1000, iter = 3000,
    chains = 4, 
    cores = 1, 
    seed = 123,
    silent = 2,
    control = list(adapt_delta = 0.99)
  )
  
  # Calculate the tail probabilities for the requested middle intervals.
  # For example, a 95% interval leaves 2.5% on each side (0.025 and 0.975).
  ci_probs <- c(0.005, 0.015, 0.025, 0.03, 0.97, 0.975, 0.985, 0.995)
  
  # Strip out just the fixed effects summaries to save memory
  results_summary <- brms::fixef(fit, probs = ci_probs) |> 
    as_tibble(rownames = "term") |> 
    mutate(outcome = outcome, .before = 1)
  
  # Extract raw posterior draws to calculate the fraction that are strictly positive.
  # Fixed effects are prefixed with 'b_' in the raw posterior dataframe.
  prob_positive <- as_draws_df(fit) |> 
    select(starts_with("b_")) |> 
    summarise(across(everything(), \(x) mean(x > 0))) |> 
    pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "term", values_to = "fraction_positive") |> 
    # Remove the 'b_' prefix so it seamlessly joins with the summary table
    mutate(term = str_remove(term, "^b_"))
    
  # Combine standard credible intervals with the fraction positive metric
  final_summary <- results_summary |> 
    left_join(prob_positive, by = "term")
  
  return(final_summary)
}

# Set up parallel processing. 
# 'workers' defines how many models run at the exact same time.
# Ensure workers * cores_per_model does not exceed total system RAM and CPU threads.
future::plan(multisession, workers = 6)

# Record the start time to track the total duration of the modeling process.
start_time <- Sys.time()

# Map the modeling function over all taxa outcomes in parallel.
# A progress bar is included to monitor the long computation.
all_models_results <- taxa_outcomes |> 
  set_names() |> 
  future_map_dfr(
    \(outcome) fit_taxa_model(outcome, meta_genus_clr, base_rhs, model_priors),
    .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE),
    .progress = TRUE
  )

# Calculate the difference and print the total execution time.
end_time <- Sys.time()
print(end_time - start_time)

all_models_results |> write_csv('../data/R63_genus_clr_all_models_results.csv')
```

## E7a heatmap

```{r}
replacement_dictionary <- setNames(key$shortname, key$fg1_name)

level_order <- rev(c(
    "abx * Sweets", "Sweets",
    "abx * Grains", "Grains",
    "abx * Milk", "Milk",
    "abx * Eggs", "Eggs",
    "abx * Legumes", "Legumes",
    "abx * Meats", "Meats",
    "abx * Fruits", "Fruits",
    "abx * Oils", "Oils",
    "abx * Vegetables", "Vegetables"
))

# Load the pre-summarized Bayesian model results
# Filter to keep only the food group terms, removing baseline intensity variables
post_summary <- read_csv('../data/R63_genus_clr_all_models_results.csv') |> 
  filter(str_detect(term, "fg_")) |> 
  mutate(
    # Clean up the term names for a prettier x-axis
    clean_term = term %>% 
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE$", "abx") %>%
      str_remove_all("empiricalFALSE:|avg_intake_|TRUE$") %>%
      str_replace_all( replacement_dictionary) %>%
      str_replace("empiricalTRUE:", "abx * ") %>%
      str_replace_all("_", " ")  ,
    
    # Assign significance marks based on tiered credible intervals
    # Check the strictest thresholds (99%) first, then cascade down to 97% and 94%
    # The quantiles reflect the leftover tails: e.g., 94% CI means 3% on each tail (Q3 and Q97)
    mark = case_when(
      Q0.5 > 0 ~ "***",
      Q99.5 < 0 ~ "***",
      Q1.5 > 0 ~ "**",
      Q98.5 < 0 ~ "**",
      Q3 > 0 ~ "*",
      Q97 < 0 ~ "*",
      .default = ""
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(clean_term = factor(clean_term, levels = level_order))
```

## dendrogram

```{r}
post_matrix <- post_summary %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(term, 'intensity')) |> 
  select(outcome, term,fraction_positive ) |> 
  spread('term','fraction_positive') %>% 
  column_to_rownames('outcome')

hc <- hclust(dist(post_matrix), method = "complete")

dendro_data <- dendro_data(hc, type = "rectangle")

# Extract the ordered genera names to ensure the heatmap aligns with the dendrogram
ordered_genera <- hc$labels[hc$order]

# Build the dendrogram plot
# Removing scale_y_reverse keeps the leaves at the 0 position on the left
# so the tree branches reach out toward the heatmap that will sit on its left
dendro_plot <- ggplot(segment(dendro_data)) + 
  geom_segment(aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend)) + 
  coord_flip() + 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0.5)) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0.5)) +
  theme_dendro()
```

```{r}
# Lock the outcome variable as a factor using the order from the clustering
heatmap_data <- post_summary |> 
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = ordered_genera))

# Build the main heatmap visualization
heatmap_plot <- heatmap_data |> 
  # make the nonsignificant ones blank to not be too overwhelming
  mutate(Estimate = if_else(!str_detect(mark , '\\*'), 0, Estimate)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = clean_term, y = outcome, fill = Estimate)) +
  geom_tile(color = "black", width = 0.95, height = 0.95) +
  geom_text(aes(label = mark), nudge_y = -0.15, size = 1.5, color = "black") +
  # Use a diverging color palette to show positive vs negative effects
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "royalblue", 
    mid = "white", 
    high = "firebrick", 
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(fill = "Effect size\n(posterior median)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_markdown(face = "italic"),
    axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = 'left', 
    panel.background = element_rect(colour = "white", fill = "white"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
heatmap_plot
```
```{r}
# Stitch the dendrogram and heatmap together side-by-side
# Putting the heatmap first naturally places it on the left
final_figure <- plot_grid(
  heatmap_plot, 
  dendro_plot, 
  ncol = 2, 
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.1),
  align = 'h', 
  axis = 'tb'
)

# Output the final figure to the console to view in the environment
final_figure

# Save the stitched figure to a PDF file
# The dimensions can be tweaked to prevent text squishing or overlapping 
ggsave(
  filename = '../data/E07a_R63_genus_heatmap.pdf',
  plot = final_figure,
  width = 140, 
  height = 95, 
  units = "mm", 
  device = 'pdf', 
  dpi = 300
)
```
